inputs.userGoal: Launch a cloud-based webhook-driven PDF converter using Azure Functions, persist job metadata in Cosmos DB, and use Azure Key Vault for secrets.

architecturePrinciples: Serverless-first for cost efficiency and auto-scaling
Secure secrets management via Key Vault with RBAC
Observability with Application Insights and Log Analytics

reasoning.whyChosen: Required backing store for workspace-based Application Insights. PerGB2018 is the recommended default SKU for new deployments.
alternativesConsidered: Free tier — limited to 500 MB/day and 7-day retention, insufficient for production
reasoning.tradeoffs: Pay-as-you-go cost scales with log volume; 30-day retention keeps costs low while providing adequate troubleshooting window.

resources.name: appi-pdfconverter-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Provides request tracing, failure diagnostics, and performance monitoring for Azure Functions. Workspace-based mode is the current recommended approach.
alternativesConsidered: Classic Application Insights — being deprecated in favor of workspace-based
reasoning.tradeoffs: Adds minor ingestion cost but essential for production observability of a webhook-driven workload.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Workspace-based Application Insights requires a Log Analytics workspace as its data store.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required by Azure Functions for trigger management, logging, and internal state. StorageV2 is mandatory for Functions (needs Queue + Table support). Standard_LRS is cost-effective for a single-region deployment.
alternativesConsidered: Standard_ZRS — zone-redundant but adds cost; not needed unless zone-redundant Functions are required
reasoning.tradeoffs: LRS provides no zone redundancy. Acceptable for a non-critical workload; upgrade to ZRS if higher availability is required.

reasoning.whyChosen: Consumption plan (Y1) provides pay-per-execution pricing with automatic scale-out, ideal for a webhook-driven workload with variable traffic patterns.
alternativesConsidered: Premium EP1 — always-warm with VNet support, but significantly higher cost for unpredictable traffic
Flex Consumption FC1 — newer consumption model with VNet support, but higher baseline cost
reasoning.tradeoffs: Cold starts may add latency to first requests (~1-3s). 5-minute default execution timeout may limit very large PDF conversions. No VNet integration on Consumption.

reasoning.whyChosen: User-assigned managed identity enables passwordless authentication from Azure Functions to Key Vault and Cosmos DB. Preferred over system-assigned for portability across deployment slots.
alternativesConsidered: System-assigned managed identity — simpler but tied to the Function App lifecycle
reasoning.tradeoffs: Requires explicit role assignments but provides consistent identity across redeployments.

resources.name: kv-pdfconverter-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Centralized secrets management for API keys, connection strings, and any third-party credentials used by the PDF converter. RBAC authorization is the modern recommended approach over access policies.
alternativesConsidered: App Configuration — suitable for non-secret config but does not replace Key Vault for sensitive data
Environment variables — insecure and not auditable
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard SKU covers software-protected keys; upgrade to Premium only if HSM-backed keys are needed. Purge protection prevents accidental permanent deletion but means deleted secrets consume quota for 90 days.

resources.dependencyReasoning: The managed identity needs to exist before RBAC role assignments can grant it access to Key Vault secrets.

reasoning.whyChosen: Serverless Cosmos DB with NoSQL API is ideal for storing job metadata (job ID, status, timestamps, output URLs). Serverless eliminates minimum throughput costs and auto-scales RU consumption per-request, well suited for webhook-driven bursty traffic. Session consistency provides read-your-writes guarantee which is sufficient for job status tracking.
alternativesConsidered: Azure SQL Database — relational model unnecessary for simple job metadata documents
Azure Table Storage — cheaper but lacks indexing, querying flexibility, and global distribution options
Provisioned Cosmos DB — unnecessary cost for variable/unpredictable traffic
reasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless is single-region only (no geo-replication). Per-request RU pricing can be expensive under sustained high throughput; upgrade to provisioned if traffic becomes predictable and constant.

resources.name: func-pdfconverter-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure Functions with HTTP trigger provides a natural webhook endpoint for PDF conversion requests. Consumption plan auto-scales to zero when idle, minimizing cost. Key Vault references in app settings enable secure secret retrieval without code changes.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Container Apps — more flexible but adds container management complexity for a simple webhook
Azure App Service — always-on cost model unnecessary for event-driven workload
reasoning.tradeoffs: Consumption plan cold starts (1-3s) may affect first-request latency. 5-minute default timeout may constrain large PDF conversions; can be extended to 10 minutes on Consumption. FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME set to dotnet-isolated as a default; change to node, python, or java as needed.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Function App requires: App Service Plan (hosting), Storage Account (runtime state), Application Insights (monitoring), Key Vault (secrets via references), Cosmos DB (job metadata), and Managed Identity (authentication).

references.title: Key Vault references in App Settings

overallReasoning.summary: Fully serverless architecture optimized for a webhook-driven PDF conversion workload. Azure Functions (Consumption) handles inbound HTTP webhooks and PDF processing. Cosmos DB (Serverless, NoSQL API) stores job metadata with pay-per-request pricing. Key Vault with RBAC secures all secrets, accessed via managed identity and Key Vault references in app settings. Application Insights backed by Log Analytics provides end-to-end observability. All resources are in eastus for co-location and lowest latency.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless-first approach trades guaranteed low-latency for significant cost savings at low-to-moderate traffic. Cold starts on both Functions and Cosmos DB may add latency to initial requests. Single-region deployment has no geo-redundancy — acceptable for non-mission-critical workloads. If PDF conversions are long-running (>10 min), consider Durable Functions or a queue-based pattern with a Dedicated/Premium plan.

plan.validation: All resources are co-located in eastus. Storage Account uses StorageV2 (required for Functions). Consumption plan does not reference VNet-secured storage. Cosmos DB serverless is single-region with no multi-region writes (consistent with EnableServerless constraint). Key Vault uses RBAC authorization with soft delete and purge protection enabled. Managed identity provides passwordless access from Functions to Key Vault and Cosmos DB. Application Insights is workspace-based with Log Analytics backing store.
